document.source_title: IPL 2026 Playoff Scenarios: What RR win over LSG would mean for qualification chances of PBKS, CSK, DC, KKR
document.summary: The Rajasthan Royals (RR) are in a crucial position as they face the Lucknow Super Giants (LSG) in their penultimate match of the IPL 2026 season. With both RR and Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) vying for the final playoff spot, a victory for RR could significantly impact the standings. Currently, RR sits at 12 points, and a win would elevate them to 14 points, pushing Punjab Kings (PBKS) down to fifth place. The article outlines the implications of an RR victory for other teams still in contention for the playoffs.
key_takeaways: RR currently has 12 points and needs a win to secure a playoff spot.
A victory for RR would push PBKS down to fifth place.
CSK and DC also need favorable outcomes to remain in contention.
KKR must win their final match to stay in the race if RR wins.
LSG is already out of playoff contention with only 8 points.
The playoff race is tight with multiple teams on equal points.
document.why_it_matters: Understanding the playoff scenarios is crucial for fans and teams as it highlights the competitive nature of the IPL and the potential shifts in standings based on match outcomes.

claims.claim_text: A victory for RR would mean they jump to 14 points and take fourth place.
claims.evidence_text: A victory for RR over LSG today would mean that they will jump up to 14 points and thus take fourth place.

claims.claim_text: PBKS will drop to fifth place if RR wins.
claims.evidence_text: If RR beat LSG, PBKS will go down to fifth place.

claims.claim_text: CSK needs to win by a large margin if RR wins.
claims.evidence_text: If RR beat LSG, CSK will have to win their final game of the season by a huge margin.

claims.claim_text: DC must win their final game to stay in contention.
claims.evidence_text: If RR beat LSG, DC simply have to win their final game of the season.

claims.claim_text: KKR will be ruled out if RR wins their last match.
claims.evidence_text: If that happen, KKR will quite simply be mathematically ruled out as they can only reach a maximum of 15 points while RR winning their last game would put them on 16.

metrics.name: Points
metrics.evidence_text: RR are sitting on 12 points in 12 matches with six wins and defeats each.

metrics.name: Net Run Rate
metrics.evidence_text: However, they are placed above both sides on fifth due to their net run rate of 0.027.
